feedback.text: I welcome this proposal whole heartedly and would like to add, as others have suggested a consumer protection measure for ownership of digital godos. Buying should mean owning a product not borrowing a product. And if you are borrowing, it should say so. But companies/publishers nowadays are treating their licenses as buying while they only give you access to the game and can revoke said access whenever they want for any given reason. And hide all of this in their fine print. Thats an abuse to consumers who think they are buying the game and thus, owning the game. I would like to propose to include the guidelines outlined by Stop Destroying Videogames to protect consumers from further abuse. Also, in relation to the censorship of the Collective SHOUT group towards the Visa, Mastercard and PayPal banks, I must say that payment processors should have no authority over platform content. Their sole responsibility should be to securely and efficiently process transactions not to dictate what consumers are allowed to purchase. Allowing financial intermediaries to become de facto censors is a dangerous act that threatens free expression, artistic freedom, and consumer choice. I believe tackling these two would be a possitive addition to the law. All digital media should be protected, and videogames are an important part of it. Thank you for your consideration.
